title: [WIP] [CI:DOCS] Script which retrieves all running podman containers on system
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
willing to heavily comment in the script starting with maybe a several line comment of what it does?
Only thing I would add is: This script seems like something that should belong under `containers/appstore/scripts/podman_ps/`. Along with a `README.md` and CI tests (I can wish can't I?) :grin:

I agree with @edsantiago concerns, we should also not run podman for users who never ran podman before. This will cause podman to create a db which means the storage paths are locked and cannot be altered later.

If we talk about running containers maybe the better starting point would be `ps -fwwC conmon` then we would actually only get users with running containers and do not need to log into all others.

>     * it is laughably unreliable (will miss containers under a different `--root` or `--database)
> 
Agreed. This would only catch users who accidentally do something wrong, not a security measure to find users actively trying to subvert the script.

>     * it affects the system, by (e.g.) mounting NFS home directories
> 
Unlikely since the session is already running.

>     * it affects the user, by (e.g.) creating `$HOME/.containers-or-config-or-something` if there wasn't one
> 
I switched to checking for conmon process, which should make this much less likely.

>     * it affects accounting: the `machinectl` session shows up under `last`.
> 
Agreed.

>     * I can't even imagine the side effects of accounts with different login shells, or strange environments
> 
I doubt this is a huge problem, and something users could adjust for.

> Only thing I would add is: This script seems like something that should belong under `containers/appstore/scripts/podman_ps/`. Along with a `README.md` and CI tests (I can wish can't I?) grin

SGTM I will close this and open over there.
